core_model.structure_summary: A complex scalar field evolves linearly and first-order along one coordinate as the transverse Laplacian with an imaginary coefficient, i.e. a free Schrodinger-type parabolic equation solved as an initial-value problem from a prescribed transverse profile.
core_model.canonical_form: i\,\partial_t u(x,y,t) = -\tfrac{1}{2}\,(\partial_x^2 + \partial_y^2)\,u(x,y,t), \qquad u(x,y,0) = e^{-(a x^2 + b y^2)}\,\mathcal{F}(x+iy)

variables.meaning: complex scalar field over a two-dimensional transverse domain
the propagation coordinate playing the role of an evolution parameter, first order
two transverse continuous coordinates entering through a Laplacian
coefficients of the anisotropic Gaussian modulation fixing the initial profile

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A complex scalar field obeys a linear first-order-in-evolution-parameter equation set equal to its transverse Laplacian with an imaginary coefficient (free Schrodinger / complex diffusion), advanced from an initial condition.

notes: The governing equation is the paraxial (free Schrodinger) PDE: transverse Laplacian plus i2k times the first derivative along the propagation axis equals zero. All subsequent operator-exponential machinery, Bessel/Airy expansions, and Cauchy-Riemann (entire-function) initial data are exact-solution techniques and specific initial conditions for this single linear PDE, not distinct models. Diffusion with an imaginary coefficient gives phase oscillation rather than pure smoothing, hence both diffusion_term and oscillatory_dynamics.
